summary.intent: Upgrade the whatsapp-rust ecosystem from 0.2 to 0.5 to fix inbound WhatsApp messages being parsed as empty due to outdated protobuf definitions, then add WhatsApp as a default offered channel across the codebase.
summary.outcome: All 6 whatsapp crates bumped to 0.5, new trait methods implemented in both sled and memory stores, WhatsApp added to default channels in 4 locations, PR #709 created and PR review comments addressed — all 4053 workspace tests passing.

repo: There are two separate default channel lists: one in crates/config/src/schema/runtime.rs (authoritative config default) and a hardcoded fallback in crates/gateway/src/state.rs used before config loads.
JS fallback channel defaults exist in both onboarding-view.js and page-channels.js and must be kept in sync with the Rust defaults.

wacore 0.5 and wacore-binary 0.5 both have default features that include 'simd', which requires unstable core::simd::Select not available on the pinned nightly — must set default-features = false on both.
whatsapp-rust must keep default-features = false to avoid sqlite-storage / libsqlite3-sys conflict (documented in a comment at Cargo.toml line 221).
The full workspace test suite uses --all-features which pulls in CUDA/llama-cpp-sys-2 on macOS and fails; tests must be run without --all-features or by excluding those crates.
Config and gateway crate tests contain hardcoded expected channel lists (channels_config_defaults_offered, channels_config_empty_toml) that must be updated when defaults change.

code.finding: default_channels_offered() is the single authoritative source for the config-layer default channel list.
Hardcoded fallback channels_offered list used before config loads; must mirror the config default.
ProtocolStore::get_skdm_recipients and add_skdm_recipients changed from String to Jid in wacore 0.5; test data using .into() must switch to .parse().unwrap().
DashMap iteration order is non-deterministic — get_latest_sync_key_id must track the latest key explicitly via a separate field rather than iterating the map.
whatsapp-rust 0.5 bot builder requires .with_runtime(TokioRuntime) call — new 4th type parameter for the async runtime.

workflow: cargo check -p <crate> is the right first tool after a major dependency version bump — reveals all breaking API changes before attempting full compilation.
When Cargo.lock has a merge conflict, the fastest resolution is to take main's lockfile and re-run cargo fetch to layer in the new dependencies.
PR review comments included: replace manual epoch math (SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap().as_secs()) with the time crate, and flag non-deterministic map iteration.

friction: wacore 0.5 and wacore-binary 0.5 default features include 'simd' which requires unstable nightly API (core::simd::Select), requiring default-features = false on both — not documented in the upgrade guide.
Full workspace --all-features test run fails on macOS due to llama-cpp-sys-2/CUDA dependency, requiring manual exclusion of those crates.
Multiple locations (config schema, gateway state, two JS files, config tests) must all be kept in sync for default channel changes — no single source of truth enforced.
open_items: Users with existing WhatsApp pairings may need to re-scan QR codes if wacore struct serialization changed between 0.2 and 0.5 — noted in PR but not yet documented in user-facing docs.
Manual QA verification pending: pair a WhatsApp device and confirm text DMs route to LLM instead of returning 'unhandled message type' error.
